name: Cuban Black Bean Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort and soak beans overnight; drain and discard the soak water.
2. In a 4-quart pot, sauté onion in butter or margarine until tender but not browned.
3. Add soaked beans, 4 cups water, bouillon cube, ham (cut into 4-6 chunks), bay leaves, thyme, oregano, salt, and red pepper.
4. Bring to a boil; reduce heat.
5. Cover and simmer until beans are tender, about 1 to 1-1/2 hours.
6. Remove 1 cup of beans from the stew and mash in a bowl with a potato masher or fork.
7. Add mashed beans back to the stew; stir to thicken.
8. Remove ham and dice it. Discard bay leaves and red pepper if used.
9. Add diced meat, green bell pepper, and rum (if desired) to the beans.
10. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
11. Serve beans over rice and top with sour cream if desired.
